title: Daily Briefing — Apr 20
headline: Tech surges, but flows and greed tell you to keep your guard up.
body: Right, so equities are ripping — SPY up 1.2% to 710, QQQ up 1.3% to 648.85, with tech and comms leading like it's AI-fueled 2021 again. Big names AAPL ($270.23), NVDA ($201.68), GOOGL ($341.68) are all cleanly above their 20 MA, which is classic momentum confirmation. But Fear & Greed has spiked from extreme fear (15.6) to greed (68.1) in just a month — that's a rapid shift, and the kind you don't chase blindly.

Sector rotation is textbook: XLK (Tech) and XLC (Comms) in the 'leading' quadrant with low risk, while XLE (Energy), XLB (Materials), XLI (Industrials), and XLU (Utilities) are rolling over. XLF (Financials) is improving, so the canary isn't gasping yet, but staples and healthcare lag — defensive sectors are not participating. Watch for rotational plays as XLY (Discretionary) picks up steam.

Contrarian lens: retail greed is flashing, breadth still looks wide, but elevated risk in materials and energy (see AA -6.8%, SM -7.5%), and the speed of this rally means you stick to the flow — not chase headlines. No breakdowns in bonds (HYG $80.65, LQD $110.04, both above their 20 MA), so macro risk isn't flashing red, but with momentum this fast, extreme retail enthusiasm is your warning, not your confirmation. Patience, react, don't predict.
callout_body:
SPY: $710.14 (20 MA: 666.62) — momentum zone
NVDA: $201.68 (20 MA: 181.44) — tech leadership
XLE: $55.02 (20 MA: 58.90) — breaking down, risk elevated
Fear & Greed: 68.1 — caution needed
AA: $65.62 (20 MA: 66.53) — materials weakness
HYG/LQD: Both above 20 MA — no macro breakdown
action_items: Stick to tech momentum: AAPL, NVDA, GOOGL, XLK above 20 MA; trail stops with the daily 20 MA
Reduce exposure to energy (XLE, AA, SM) and materials; wait for mean reversion or sector improvement
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Abundance mindset — next trade, not last chase.
